bhattavetanānuppadānena.gram: neuter noun, instrumental singular, compound (bhattavetana + anuppadāna)
breakdown: By providing, by giving regularly; from anu + pa + √dā.

bhavanetti.meaning: the conduit to becoming; what leads to renewed existence

bhavataṇhā.meaning: craving for becoming; craving for repeated existence
bhavataṇhā.summary: This is the second of the three cravings named in the Second Noble Truth.
bhavataṇhā.goenka: Bhavataṇhā, the craving for becoming, for repeated rebirth, is one of the three forms of craving (taṇhā) named in the Noble Truth of the Arising of Suffering, together with kāmataṇhā, the craving for sensual pleasures, and vibhavataṇhā, the craving for annihilation. It is this craving, occurring again and again and finding delight now here, now there, that keeps the round of suffering turning.

bhañjeyya.gram: verb, optative 3rd person singular of bhañjati (√bhañj)

bhaññamāne.meaning: while being spoken; as it was being told, preached
bhaññamāne.gram: present participle (passive) of bhaṇati, locative singular (locative absolute)

bhaṇantiyā.gram: present participle of bhaṇati, feminine genitive/instrumental singular

bhaṇāmahe.gram: verb, imperative/middle 1st person plural of bhaṇati (√bhaṇ)

bhaṇḍanajātā.meaning: fallen into quarrelling; in whom strife has arisen
breakdown: Produced, born, arisen; past participle of jāyati.

bhaṇḍanakalahaviggahavivādā.gram: masculine noun, plural, compound (bhaṇḍana + kalaha + viggaha + vivāda)

bhedapurekkhāratā.meaning: preferring division; taking pleasure in causing breach

bhedā.meaning: breakage; from the breaking up (as in kāyassa bhedā, “from the breaking up of the body”)
bhedā.gram: masculine noun, ablative singular of bheda (√bhid)

bhettā.gram: masculine agent noun, nominative singular of bhettar (from bhedeti)
breakdown: Root “to break” (bhed- in the agent stem).
Agent noun ending (bhettar, nominative bhettā): “one who breaks”.

bhikkhave.gram: masculine noun, vocative plural of bhikkhu (irregular)
bhikkhave.goenka: Bhikkhave, here left untranslated as “Bhikkhus”, is the term the Buddha generally used to address his assembly. That assembly often consisted not only of monks and nuns but of laypeople as well; the address therefore reaches everyone who has gathered to hear and practise the teaching.

bhindeyya.gram: verb, optative 3rd person singular of bhindati (√bhid)

bhindissati.meaning: he will break; he will split
bhindissati.gram: verb, future 3rd person singular of bhindati (√bhid)

bhinditabbo.meaning: to be divided; that should be split
bhinditabbo.gram: potential participle, nominative singular masculine, of bhindati (√bhid + itabba)

bhiyyobhāvāya.gram: masculine abstract noun, dative singular, compound (bhiyyo + bhāva)
breakdown: For the state, for the making; dative singular of bhāva.

bho.meaning: friend; sir (a familiar address used for equals and inferiors)
bho.goenka: Bho, literally “friend” or “sir”, is an address used for equals and inferiors. In the suttas its use is telling: when Subha addresses the Enlightened One as bho gotama, it shows that at that stage he did not yet have full respect towards the Enlightenment; only after the Buddha's exposition did he take full refuge.

bhogakkhandhaṁ.meaning: a mass of wealth; an accumulation of riches
bhogakkhandhaṁ.gram: masculine noun, accusative singular, compound (bhoga + khandha)
breakdown: Wealth, possessions (with sandhi doubling of the following k).

bhoti.meaning: dear madam; you (respectful-familiar address to a woman)

breakdown: Much, greatly (with sandhi doubling of the following p).
Careless, negligent; past participle of pamajjati.

bhuñjamānānaṁ.meaning: of those eating; of those enjoying
bhuñjamānānaṁ.gram: present participle of bhuñjati, genitive plural

bhāgamakāsiṁ.meaning: I made a portion; I divided into shares
bhāgamakāsiṁ.gram: verb, aorist 1st person singular, compound (bhāgaṁ + akāsiṁ)
breakdown: Portion, share (the m bridges in sandhi).
I made, I did; aorist of karoti.

bhāratayuddhasītāharaṇādiniratthakakathā.meaning: preference for meaningless talk such as the Bhārata war, the abduction of Sītā and the like
bhāratayuddhasītāharaṇādiniratthakakathā.summary: This long compound describes the fondness for frivolous worldly storytelling (the epics) as a kind of pointless talk.

bhāsissāmī.gram: verb, future 1st person singular of bhāsati (√bhās)

bhāsitaṁ.meaning: that which is spoken; the saying, the utterance
bhāsitaṁ.gram: neuter noun, past participle of bhāsati (√bhās + ita)

bhāvanā.meaning: mental cultivation; meditation development; sustained practice
bhāvanā.summary: Bhāvanā is sustained meditative cultivation — actually developing a quality in the mind, not merely thinking about it.
bhāvanā.gram: feminine noun (causative of √bhū “to be, become” + -anā: “causing to become, developing”)
breakdown: Root “to be, become”; the causative stem bhāve- means “to cause to become, to develop”.
Action-noun suffix: the act of cultivating, developing.
bhāvanā.goenka: Bhāvanā is mental cultivation — meditation as the sustained development of the mind, not mere thinking about, visualisation or imagination. The word appears throughout the texts in compounds naming the cultivation of specific qualities: mettābhāvanā, karuṇābhāvanā, muditābhāvanā, upekkhābhāvanā, asubhabhāvanā, aniccasaññābhāvanā, ānāpānassatibhāvanā. This pattern shows bhāvanā as sustained meditative cultivation in which a quality is actually developed in the mind. The tradition distinguishes bhāvanāmayā paññā — wisdom arising from direct meditative experience — as the highest of the three kinds of wisdom, the kind that Vipassana develops, beyond wisdom gained by hearing or by intellectual reflection.

bhāvanānuyogamanuyuttā.meaning: engaged in the practice of mental development; devoted to the pursuit of bhāvanā
breakdown: Mental cultivation, meditation development (bhāvanā + anu- coalesces to bhāvanānu-).
Practice of, application to (the m bridges in sandhi).
Engaged in, devoted to; past participle of anuyuñjati.

bhāvaye.gram: verb, optative 3rd person singular of bhāveti (causative of bhavati)
breakdown: Causative stem of bhavati: “to cause to become, to cultivate”.

bhāvessati.gram: verb, future 3rd person singular of bhāveti (causative of bhavati)

bhāvetabban.meaning: should be developed; must be cultivated (of the path: “to be developed”)
bhāvetabban.gram: potential participle of bhāveti (with -n for -ṁ before ti in quotation sandhi)

bhāvitan.meaning: has been developed; is fulfilled through cultivation
bhāvitan.gram: past participle of bhāveti (with -n for -ṁ before ti in quotation sandhi)

bhāvitā.gram: past participle of bhāveti (causative of bhavati)

bhāṇaye.meaning: should cause to speak; may one have (it) recited
bhāṇaye.gram: verb, optative 3rd person singular, causative of bhaṇati (√bhaṇ)

bhītassa.meaning: of one who is frightened; for the fearful
bhītassa.gram: past participle of bhāyati (√bhī + ta), genitive singular
breakdown: Frightened; past participle of bhāyati “to fear”.

bhūmiladdhaṁ.meaning: having gained a footing; obtained at that stage or state
breakdown: Achieved, gained, obtained; past participle of labhati.
